table_semantics.justification: Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, and <th scope='col'> on every header cell. Status values are conveyed via visible text labels inside badges, not by colour alone. Table is not used for layout.

element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: for the search field, native

form_semantics.justification: All three inputs have explicit <label> elements with matching for/id pairs. Controls are wrapped in both <search> (landmark) and <form method='get'>, which is correct. Appropriate input type ('search') used. No placeholder-as-label pattern.

aria_discipline.justification: Mostly disciplined: <search> and <nav> are both labelled, no redundant role attributes. Minor issue: aria-current='page' is placed on the <li> element rather than the <a> inside it; assistive technologies expect aria-current on the interactive element itself. Additionally, the Previous <button> already has visible text 'Previous', making the aria-label slightly redundant (though it adds clarifying context).

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Employee Directory') on the page with no skipped levels. No headings are used decoratively for visual sizing.

landmark_structure.justification: Appropriate landmarks used throughout: <header>, <main id='main-content'>, <search aria-label='…'>, <nav aria-label='…'>, and <footer>. Skip link targets <main> and is visually hidden until focused. No landmark overuse.

content_realism.justification: Eight rows of realistic employees with diverse names, varied departments, roles, and start dates spanning 2015–2022. All three status values are represented. No placeholder or Lorem ipsum content.

list_semantics.justification: Pagination controls are correctly structured as a <ul> inside <nav>. No other collections on the page require list markup.

expectations_met: Status column content is not conveyed by colour alone — visible text labels present inside badges
Department and status filters use native <select> elements
Filter controls are grouped in both a <search> element and a <form>
Previous is a <button>, Next is an <a> — neither is a div
Table is not wrapped in role='grid'
No CSS display properties strip table semantics
expectations_missed: aria-current='page' is placed on the <li> rather than on the <a> element; assistive technologies process aria-current more reliably on the interactive element itself
additional_observations: Excellent use of <time datetime='…'> for all start-date cells
Skip link implemented correctly with CSS transform hide/show on focus
Using both <search> (HTML landmark element) and <form> together is well-structured and idiomatic
Disabled Previous button correctly uses the disabled attribute alongside pointer-events: none
